heading: Chess Club
text:
How do I join? Email the PTA at jerseycityps5pta@gmail.com and we'll get you connected.
When do we meet? Fridays at 5pm in the school garden (or Gia Gelato if the weather is bad). Goal is once a week.
Rules during a meetup: everyone plays; if someone asks you to play them, try to say yes; set up the board after you finish a game; don't touch other people's pieces; be kind.
Can I drop off my kindergartener? No — they need to be old enough to self-release from CASPER, since Chess Club doesn't supervise young children independently.
How can I help? Show up, teach kids to play, volunteer to bring snacks, help set up before 4pm, help clean up after. If you know a good indoor spot for winter, that would be great too.
Run by PTA secretary Jon Huang. Reach the PTA at jerseycityps5pta@gmail.com with any questions.

heading: Culture Club
text:
Team: Dana Patton and Raquel D'Apice.
Mission: To cultivate an environment where individuals are kind, compassionate, and understanding toward one another.
Focused projects: Base Boxes; 2nd Graders & BBBS Mentoring Program — Rocks Projects; Grade 7 STEMOTIONAL Kindness App; Champion Readers Program (Kindness, Friendship, Empathy); Kindness Challenge, Pennies for Patients Coin Drive, UNICEF, Make-A-Wish, and St. Jude's Children's Hospital collections.
To join, email ps5CKT@gmail.com.

heading: Garden Club
text:
Who can join: Middle school grades — no experience needed.
When & where: Every Wednesday, 3–4pm, school garden.
Each week members plant and tend seasonal flowers, vegetables, and herbs; learn about transplanting and composting; do nature-inspired crafts; and explore NJ natives, urban tree canopy, pollinators, birds, and insects.

heading: Music Theatre
text: The Dr. Michael Conti PS5 Theater Club offers musical theater to students in grades 4–8, culminating in a live performance. Rehearsals led by Ms. Valentin engage students creatively through singing, acting, set design, and costume making.

heading: Financial Literacy Club
text: Details coming soon. For direct information, reach out to the PTA at jerseycityps5pta@gmail.com.
We support enrichment through art residencies, school assemblies, and programs that promote inclusion, advocacy, and a love of reading.

heading: Art Residencies: Theatre
text:
Theatre with Speranza Theatre Company (K & 1st grade).
Students work with Speranza right in their classrooms through theater games and creative writing, expressing their unique voices and building cultural exposure.
Speranza is a nonprofit, women-led NJ Theater Alliance member. The PS5 PTA raises funds to support this program — email jerseycityps5pta@gmail.com to join the committee.

heading: Art Residencies: Dance
text:
Dance with Segunda Quimbamba Folkloric Center (Pre-K).
Pre-K students learn the rhythms, movements, and cultural traditions of Afro-Puerto Rican dance and music, building coordination, confidence, and cultural awareness.

heading: School Assemblies
text: PS5 hosts a variety of engaging assemblies including the Planetarium, Liberty Science Center (LSC), Rutgers Science Bus, Lizard Guy and a magician, Sk8 4 Life (wellness and resilience through skateboarding and storytelling), and middle school assemblies on anti-racism and internet safety.

heading: Book Fair
text: The PS5 PTA partners with the school for the annual Book Fair, fostering a love of reading and raising classroom funds. Our Books for the Classroom initiative lets families contribute directly to classroom supplies, snacks, and special projects.

heading: PS5 Little Free Library
text: Located at the main entrance. Bring books for kids — borrow and give freely!

heading: Invited Speakers
text: The PS5 PTA brings in guest speakers — authors, scientists, artists, and community leaders — to connect classroom subjects to real-world applications and spark curiosity for middle school students.

heading: Parent Workshops
text: Workshops on internet safety, anti-racism in education, and neurodiversity — supporting an informed, inclusive, and compassionate school community.

heading: Advocacy Committee
text: We are looking for parents and teachers to become participants at the board and city level. If you are interested, please email jerseycityps5pta@gmail.com.

heading: Special Education
text: Volunteer coordinator needed! We are looking for parents who can research applicable grants, notify the school, and help teachers write and submit them. Email jerseycityps5pta@gmail.com if interested.
This section is just getting started — add rows below with Section set to "Middle School" for middle-school-specific clubs, initiatives, and resources. See Sheet1 for instructions.
